title: Annual Aquascaping Competition
content:
Join us for our prestigious Annual Aquascaping Competition, where aquarium enthusiasts from across the region showcase their artistic talents and technical skills in creating stunning underwater landscapes.

Event Details

This year's competition features three categories:

  • Nano Tank (under 10 gallons)
  • Medium Tank (10-40 gallons)
  • Large Tank (over 40 gallons)

Participants will have their aquascapes judged by a panel of industry experts based on creativity, technical execution, plant health, and overall aesthetic appeal. Winners in each category will receive valuable prizes including AquaLuxe store credit, premium equipment, and recognition in our quarterly magazine.

Schedule

  • 9:00 AM - Registration and setup
  • 11:00 AM - Judging begins
  • 1:00 PM - Public viewing
  • 3:00 PM - Awards ceremony
  • 4:00 PM - Networking reception

Whether you're competing or simply attending to admire the artistry, this event offers a wonderful opportunity to connect with fellow hobbyists and gain inspiration for your own aquarium projects.

Registration for competitors is required by September 15th. Spectators are welcome with standard admission.
excerpt: Showcase your aquascaping talents at our annual competition featuring multiple categories, expert judging, and valuable prizes for winners.
event_location: AquaLuxe Main Store, 1234 Aquarium Way, Coral City

title: Beginner's Aquarium Workshop
content:
Starting your first aquarium? Join our comprehensive workshop designed specifically for beginners! This hands-on session will guide you through everything you need to know to successfully set up and maintain your first aquatic ecosystem.

Workshop Topics

  • Understanding the nitrogen cycle and biological filtration
  • Selecting appropriate equipment for your needs and budget
  • Choosing compatible fish species for community tanks
  • Basic water chemistry and testing procedures
  • Establishing live plants in your aquarium
  • Regular maintenance routines and schedules
  • Troubleshooting common beginner issues

Each participant will receive a starter kit including a water test kit, water conditioner, beneficial bacteria starter, and a comprehensive beginner's guide to take home. The workshop includes both classroom instruction and hands-on practice with actual aquarium setups.

What to Expect

Our experienced instructors will walk you through the entire process of setting up a freshwater aquarium, from selecting the right location to introducing your first fish. You'll have the opportunity to ask questions specific to your situation and receive personalized advice.

Space is limited to ensure individual attention, so early registration is recommended. Participants receive a 15% discount on any purchases made in our store on the day of the workshop.
excerpt: Learn the essentials of aquarium keeping in this hands-on workshop designed for beginners. Includes practical demonstrations and a starter kit to take home.
event_location: AquaLuxe Education Center, 1234 Aquarium Way, Coral City

title: Advanced Reef Keeping Seminar
content:
Elevate your reef keeping skills with our advanced seminar led by marine biology expert Dr. Marina Coralson. This intensive session focuses on the complex aspects of maintaining thriving reef ecosystems and solving challenging reef aquarium issues.

Seminar Topics

  • Advanced water chemistry and parameter stability
  • Coral nutrition and supplementation strategies
  • Innovative filtration techniques for reef systems
  • Managing coral aggression and compatibility
  • Lighting spectrum optimization for different coral species
  • Pest and disease identification and treatment
  • Breeding projects and propagation techniques

This seminar goes beyond basic reef keeping, diving into the scientific principles behind successful reef aquariums. Dr. Coralson will share insights from her 20+ years of experience in both research and home reef keeping.

Who Should Attend

This seminar is designed for intermediate to advanced hobbyists who already maintain reef aquariums and want to deepen their understanding and improve their results. Basic knowledge of reef keeping principles is assumed.

Attendees will receive a comprehensive reference guide, water testing vouchers, and exclusive access to a private online forum for continued learning and support after the seminar.
excerpt: Deepen your understanding of reef aquarium science with expert Dr. Marina Coralson in this advanced seminar covering complex reef keeping topics.
event_location: Marine Science Center, 500 Ocean Drive, Coral City

title: Monthly Fish Auction
content:
Join fellow aquarium enthusiasts for our popular Monthly Fish Auction! This community event brings together hobbyists, breeders, and collectors to buy, sell, and trade a wide variety of fish, plants, and aquarium supplies.

Event Format

The auction follows a traditional format with items presented one at a time for bidding. All items come from local hobbyists and breeders, offering unique specimens often unavailable in retail stores.

Typical auction items include:

  • Locally bred fish species
  • Home-grown aquatic plants
  • Coral frags from hobbyist tanks
  • Handmade aquarium decorations
  • Gently used equipment
  • Specialty foods and supplements

Participation Guidelines

For Sellers:

  • Registration opens at 10:00 AM
  • All items must be properly bagged/packaged and labeled
  • Sellers receive 80% of the final bid price
  • Maximum of 20 items per seller

For Buyers:

  • Viewing begins at 11:00 AM
  • Auction starts at 12:00 PM
  • Cash and credit cards accepted
  • Bring your own bags/containers for transport

This event is a great opportunity to find rare specimens, meet other hobbyists, and learn from experienced aquarists in our community. Refreshments will be available for purchase.
excerpt: Buy, sell, and trade fish, plants, and supplies at our community auction featuring unique specimens from local breeders and hobbyists.
event_location: AquaLuxe Community Hall, 1234 Aquarium Way, Coral City
